domains.name: Preparing and Using Data for Analysis

questions.stem: A company needs near-real-time ingestion of application events with durable decoupling between producers and consumers. Which Google Cloud service is the best fit?

options.explanation: Pub/Sub is the managed messaging service for decoupled event ingestion on Google Cloud. The other options do not provide the same streaming messaging role.

questions.stem: A team needs a globally consistent relational database with horizontal scale and SQL support. Which product should they choose?

options.explanation: Cloud Spanner provides globally distributed relational storage with strong consistency and SQL. Bigtable is NoSQL, and the others are not relational databases.

questions.stem: Which TWO factors are most important when choosing between BigQuery and Bigtable? (Select TWO)

options.text: Analytical SQL versus low-latency key-value access patterns
options.explanation: BigQuery is optimized for analytical SQL, while Bigtable is optimized for low-latency key-based access. Browser tabs and logo color are irrelevant.

options.text: Expected query style and access pattern
options.explanation: BigQuery is optimized for analytical SQL, while Bigtable is optimized for low-latency key-based access. Browser tabs and logo color are irrelevant.

options.text: The number of browser tabs the team uses
Whether the company has a logo in blue

questions.stem: A retailer wants one architecture for both streaming click events and historical reprocessing using the same business logic. Which service is MOST aligned?

options.explanation: Apache Beam on Dataflow provides a unified programming model for batch and streaming, which is ideal when the same logic must support both modes.

questions.stem: Why are partitioning and clustering often used together in BigQuery?

options.text: They reduce the amount of data scanned for many analytical queries
options.explanation: Partitioning and clustering help BigQuery scan less data and improve performance/cost efficiency for many query patterns. They do not replace IAM or schema design.

options.text: They eliminate the need for schema design

questions.stem: What is a common reason to store raw landing-zone files in Cloud Storage?

options.text: It provides durable, low-cost object storage for many file formats
options.explanation: Cloud Storage is a common low-cost landing zone for raw files. BigQuery can read many sources, and Cloud Storage does not automatically normalize schemas or replace governance.

options.text: It is the only place BigQuery can read from

questions.stem: A data platform team wants a managed governance layer for discovering, governing, and monitoring data across zones. Which service is MOST relevant?

options.explanation: Dataplex is the relevant managed governance and data management layer across data lakes and warehouses. The other services address different domains.

questions.stem: A workload must serve sub-10 ms lookups for user profiles keyed by ID at massive scale. Why is BigQuery a poor primary store for this use case?

options.text: BigQuery is optimized for analytics, not ultra-low-latency key-based serving workloads
options.explanation: BigQuery is an analytical warehouse, not a low-latency serving database. It supports SQL and string data, and Google manages the infrastructure.

questions.stem: What is the main benefit of a Pub/Sub dead-letter topic?

options.text: Messages that repeatedly fail delivery can be isolated for investigation
options.explanation: Dead-letter topics capture repeatedly undeliverable messages for inspection and remediation. They do not by themselves provide exactly-once semantics, replace ack handling, or automatically configure CMEK.

options.text: It makes every subscriber exactly-once by itself
It encrypts all payloads with customer-managed keys automatically

questions.stem: A streaming pipeline reads from Pub/Sub and writes to BigQuery. Which service is best suited to apply event-time windowing and streaming transforms?

options.explanation: Dataflow is the managed Beam runtime for streaming transformations including event-time windows. The other services are not the standard streaming transform engine for this pattern.

questions.stem: Which TWO ingestion services are commonly used for moving data into BigQuery from managed sources? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: BigQuery Data Transfer Service and Datastream are relevant managed ingestion tools. IAP and Artifact Registry solve different problems.

options.text: Datastream for change data capture scenarios
options.explanation: BigQuery Data Transfer Service and Datastream are relevant managed ingestion tools. IAP and Artifact Registry solve different problems.

questions.stem: A Dataflow job shows growing watermark lag because many events arrive late and out of order. Which remediation is MOST targeted?

options.text: Review event-time windowing, allowed lateness, and trigger behavior
options.explanation: Watermark lag is closely tied to event-time behavior, lateness, and trigger configuration. Dataset count and Cloud Functions do not address the root cause.

options.text: Increase the number of BigQuery datasets
Convert the job to Cloud Functions

questions.stem: Why might a team choose Dataproc over Dataflow for a batch transformation?

options.text: They need managed Spark/Hadoop compatibility for an existing workload
options.explanation: Dataproc is the managed Spark/Hadoop service and is often chosen for compatibility with existing ecosystem workloads. The other options mismatch the requirement.

options.text: They want to avoid all clusters and still run Spark unchanged

questions.stem: Which service is most appropriate for physically transferring very large on-premises datasets when network transfer is impractical?

options.explanation: Transfer Appliance is designed for offline bulk data transfer when network migration is impractical. The other services are unrelated.

questions.stem: A streaming ingestion design must absorb temporary consumer outages without losing messages. Which Pub/Sub property matters most?

options.text: Durable decoupling between publishers and subscribers
options.explanation: Pub/Sub decouples producers and consumers durably so subscribers can recover after outages. The other options are not core Pub/Sub properties.

options.text: Automatic BigQuery partition creation in every case

questions.stem: A Beam pipeline performs a huge group-by and workers hit memory pressure. Which change is a strong first mitigation?

options.text: Use managed shuffle features and review key skew
options.explanation: Large group-by stages often benefit from managed shuffle and skew review. Renaming topics or disabling autoscaling does not address shuffle pressure.

options.text: Store outputs in local files only

questions.stem: Which BigQuery feature is most directly used to organize data by date so queries can scan only relevant slices?

options.explanation: Partitioned tables divide data into partitions, often by date/time, so queries can scan only relevant slices. The other features serve unrelated purposes.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of clustering in BigQuery?

options.text: To colocate related rows based on clustered columns for more efficient query pruning
options.explanation: Clustering organizes storage by clustered columns so BigQuery can scan less data for qualifying predicates. It does not create subscriptions, cross-cloud replication, or schemas.

options.text: To replicate data across clouds automatically

questions.stem: Which TWO Bigtable schema design practices are usually recommended? (Select TWO)

options.text: Design row keys to distribute traffic and avoid hotspotting
options.explanation: Bigtable schemas should reflect access patterns and avoid hotspotting through good key design. Giant blobs and SQL joins are poor default assumptions.

options.text: Keep access patterns in mind when designing keys and families
options.explanation: Bigtable schemas should reflect access patterns and avoid hotspotting through good key design. Giant blobs and SQL joins are poor default assumptions.

options.text: Use random giant blobs in one column family for every workload
Assume SQL joins will be the main access path

questions.stem: A team stores high-cardinality time-series telemetry and needs predictable millisecond reads by key. Which store is usually the better fit than BigQuery?

options.explanation: Bigtable is designed for very large-scale low-latency key-based reads and writes. BigQuery is optimized for analytics, not primary millisecond serving.

questions.stem: Why are materialized views useful in BigQuery?

options.text: They can speed up repeated queries by precomputing and maintaining results for supported patterns
options.explanation: Materialized views can improve performance for repeated query patterns by maintaining precomputed results. They do not replace all base tables or create messaging resources.

options.text: They are only for exporting CSV files

questions.stem: When would external tables over Cloud Storage be attractive?

options.text: When you want to query supported files in place before or instead of loading them fully
options.explanation: External tables are useful for querying supported files in place. They are not transactional serving stores and do not provide HBase APIs.

options.text: When you need sub-millisecond transactional updates
When you want to avoid object storage completely

questions.stem: A team needs to retain raw immutable files and curated analytics tables. Which combination fits well?

options.text: Cloud Storage for raw files and BigQuery for curated analytics
options.explanation: Cloud Storage is a common raw landing zone, and BigQuery is a common curated analytics store. The other combinations do not fit the requirement.

options.text: Bigtable for dashboards and Gmail for raw files
Pub/Sub for long-term storage and Dataflow for SQL serving

questions.stem: Why can a poorly chosen Bigtable row key cause severe performance problems?

options.text: Sequential or skewed keys can create hotspots on a narrow part of the keyspace
options.explanation: Poor key distribution can hotspot tablets and hurt latency and throughput. The other statements are incorrect.

options.text: It turns Bigtable into a columnar warehouse

questions.stem: What does BigQuery ML allow analysts and engineers to do?

options.text: Create and use certain ML models directly with SQL in BigQuery
options.explanation: BigQuery ML enables model creation and inference for supported ML workflows directly in SQL. It does not replace every other ML service or unrelated data products.

options.text: Replace Vertex AI in every scenario

questions.stem: A BI team repeatedly filters by customer segment and month. What data-modeling choice in BigQuery can help most?

options.text: Use partitioning and clustering aligned to common predicates
options.explanation: Aligning partitioning and clustering to common predicates helps BigQuery prune scanned data. The other choices do not optimize query access.

options.text: Store the table as one unsorted daily export forever

questions.stem: Which TWO steps improve data readiness for downstream analytics? (Select TWO)

options.text: Define clear schemas and data quality expectations
options.explanation: Explicit schemas/quality expectations and analytical modeling both improve data readiness. Accidental naming and undocumented transformations increase confusion.

options.text: Model tables to match important analytical access patterns
options.explanation: Explicit schemas/quality expectations and analytical modeling both improve data readiness. Accidental naming and undocumented transformations increase confusion.

options.text: Rely on accidental field naming conventions only

questions.stem: A team wants a semantic layer of trusted definitions and governed assets across lake and warehouse data. Which service aligns best?

options.explanation: Dataplex is the relevant governed data management layer. The other services address networking or infrastructure placement.

questions.stem: Why should transformation logic be documented and versioned?

options.text: It helps teams reproduce datasets and understand changes over time
options.explanation: Documented, versioned transformation logic supports reproducibility and team understanding. It does not remove optimization or security needs.

options.text: It forces every query to be free

questions.stem: What is a good reason to create curated tables instead of pointing every dashboard at raw files?

options.text: Curated tables improve consistency, performance, and semantic clarity for analytics
options.explanation: Curated tables provide cleaner semantics and often better performance for analytics than raw landing files. The other claims are false.

options.text: Curated tables remove all storage cost

questions.stem: A team is deciding between BigQuery ML and Vertex AI for an initial forecasting prototype already centered on SQL analysts. Which first choice is reasonable?

options.text: Start with BigQuery ML because the workflow stays close to SQL and warehouse data
options.explanation: For SQL-centric teams and supported model types, BigQuery ML is a sensible first choice. The other options add unnecessary complexity or omit evaluation.

options.text: Start by building a custom distributed training cluster manually

questions.stem: A data mart contains columns that almost no analytical query uses, but scans remain expensive. Which action is MOST targeted?

options.text: Review table design and query patterns to reduce unnecessary scanned data, including partition and cluster choices
options.explanation: Expensive scans should lead to storage/query design review, especially partitioning, clustering, and table shape. Cosmetic or governance changes do not solve scan cost.

questions.stem: Why is monitoring important for production data pipelines?

options.text: It helps detect failures, regressions, and cost issues before they become larger incidents
options.explanation: Monitoring is essential for operational visibility. It complements rather than replaces design, testing, and IAM.

options.text: It removes the need for IAM

questions.stem: What is a key benefit of Dataproc autoscaling for cluster-based batch workloads?

options.text: It can adjust cluster size to workload demand and improve cost efficiency
options.explanation: Autoscaling helps match cluster resources to workload demand, which can improve efficiency. It does not replace Spark semantics or guarantee no failures.

options.text: It turns Dataproc into a serverless message queue
It replaces Spark jobs with SQL views

questions.stem: Which TWO signals are valuable to monitor for a production analytics platform? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Operational failures/lag and cost/resource trends are important production signals. Font preferences and color schemes are not.

options.text: Query cost and resource consumption trends
options.explanation: Operational failures/lag and cost/resource trends are important production signals. Font preferences and color schemes are not.

questions.stem: A pipeline change improves speed but produces subtly wrong aggregations. What does this illustrate?

options.text: Operational performance improvements must still be validated for correctness
options.explanation: Performance gains do not guarantee correctness, so data quality validation remains necessary. The other statements are unsafe assumptions.

options.text: Faster pipelines are always more accurate
Data quality checks are optional in batch systems

questions.stem: Why should teams automate repeatable pipeline deployment steps?

options.text: Automation reduces drift and makes releases more reproducible
options.explanation: Automation reduces inconsistency and improves reproducibility. It does not eliminate reviews or guarantee no downtime.

options.text: Automation removes the need for review

questions.stem: A job succeeds technically but arrives after the business SLA. What category of issue is this?

options.text: A reliability/operational issue because timeliness is part of service quality
options.explanation: Meeting SLAs is part of production reliability. Late data can be as harmful as failed data for business outcomes.

options.text: Not an issue because the query eventually finished

questions.stem: What is a strong reason to keep representative test datasets for pipeline changes?

options.text: They help catch regressions before production rollout
options.explanation: Representative test datasets help surface regressions pre-release. They complement monitoring and source control rather than replace them.

options.text: They remove the need for monitoring
They guarantee no schema changes ever occur

questions.stem: A team has many independent data products and wants a consistent governance and operational view across them. Which service helps most?

options.explanation: Dataplex provides a consistent governance-oriented view across distributed data assets. The other services do not serve that role.
